BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Increase in danger of wet avalanches in the course of the day.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches. In particular at the base of rock walls avalanches can be released naturally.
Individual avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2500 m. Such avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Some snow fell on Tuesday in some regions.
The surface of the snowpack will soften during the day. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to increasing and thorough wetting of the snowpack below approximately 3000 m.
Steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2500 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack.
Below approximately 2000 m from a snow sport perspective, in most cases insufficient snow is lying.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Friday: The avalanche danger will persist.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Slight increase in danger of wet avalanches in the course of the day.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be only a slight increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Wet avalanches can in isolated cases be released by a single winter sport participant. This applies on very steep shady slopes below approximately 2800 m, as well as on very steep sunny slopes at elevated altitudes.

Individual avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2500 m. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be quite good. The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften during the day. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to gradual moistening of the snowpack at intermediate and high altitudes.

Shady slopes above approximately 2500 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack at elevated altitudes.

On steep sunny slopes as well as below approximately 1800 m hardly any snow is lying.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The danger of wet avalanches will persist.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Slight increase in danger of wet avalanches in the course of the day.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be only a slight increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Wet avalanches can in isolated cases be released by a single winter sport participant. This applies on very steep shady slopes below approximately 2800 m, as well as on very steep sunny slopes at elevated altitudes.

Individual avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2500 m. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be quite good. The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften during the day. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to gradual moistening of the snowpack at intermediate and high altitudes.

Shady slopes above approximately 2500 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack at elevated altitudes.

On steep sunny slopes as well as below approximately 1800 m hardly any snow is lying.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The danger of wet avalanches will persist.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: As a consequence of solar radiation the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: Wet and gliding avalanches are possible. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and in gullies and bowls. Places where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are especially precarious. The avalanches can be released in deep layers of the snowpack. Backcountry tours should be concluded early.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Some snow has fallen in some regions.
Given the weather conditions, the snow cover will remain impregnated. Snow cover and thickness vary greatly depending on exposure and altitude, and are steadily decreasing. Individual weak layers exist in the snowpack on shady slopes. The weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Warming. The weather will be sunny.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust only at high altitudes and will soften during the day. Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack on steep shady slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on steep shady slopes above approximately 2800 m.
As a consequence of warming during the day and the solar radiation, the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase in particular on steep shady slopes below approximately 2800 m.
Medium-sized avalanches are possible.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The surface of the snowpack will soften during the day.
Steep shady slopes above approximately 2800 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack.
Below approximately 2100 m from a snow sport perspective, in most cases insufficient snow is lying.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Wet and gliding snow require caution.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: In all aspects more medium-sized gliding avalanches and wet snow slides are possible especially in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: Below approximately 1800 m a little snow is lying. In gullies and bowls at high altitude there is still a very large amount of snow. The spring-like weather conditions gave rise to increasing and thorough wetting of the snowpack. A clear night: As a consequence of low temperatures a crust will form on the surface. The surface of the snowpack will already soften in the late morning.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Increase in danger of wet avalanches in the course of the day.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
In the late morning a favourable avalanche situation will be encountered over a wide area.

As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be only a slight increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Wet avalanches can in isolated cases be released by a single winter sport participant. This applies on very steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes below approximately 2500 m, as well as on very steep west and east facing slopes below approximately 2800 m, but in isolated cases also on very steep sunny slopes at elevated altitudes.

Avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2500 m. These places are very rare but are difficult to recognise.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be quite good. The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften during the day. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to gradual moistening of the snowpack at intermediate and high altitudes.

Shady slopes above approximately 2500 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack at elevated altitudes.

On steep sunny slopes as well as below approximately 1800 m hardly any snow is lying.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be quite good. The danger of wet avalanches will increase a little during the day.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Increase in danger of wet avalanches in the course of the day.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
In the late morning a favourable avalanche situation will be encountered over a wide area.

As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be only a slight increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Wet avalanches can in isolated cases be released by a single winter sport participant. This applies on very steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes below approximately 2500 m, as well as on very steep west and east facing slopes below approximately 2800 m, but in isolated cases also on very steep sunny slopes at elevated altitudes.

Avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2500 m. These places are very rare but are difficult to recognise.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be quite good. The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften during the day. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to gradual moistening of the snowpack at intermediate and high altitudes.

Shady slopes above approximately 2500 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack at elevated altitudes.

On steep sunny slopes as well as below approximately 1800 m hardly any snow is lying.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be quite good. The danger of wet avalanches will increase a little during the day.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust only at high altitudes and will already soften in the late morning.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The surface of the snowpack will only just freeze and will soften during the day.
In isolated cases the avalanches are small.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: As a consequence of mild temperatures the snowpack consolidated. As a consequence of warming during the day there will be an increase in the danger of moist and wet avalanches.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: Only isolated wet avalanches are possible. Caution is to be exercised on extremely steep shady slopes. Mostly avalanches are rather small.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften during the day.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Low avalanche danger will prevail. Wet snow requires caution.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Individual avalanche prone locations are to be found in high Alpine regions.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: Currently there are favourable conditions mostly. In these regions the avalanche prone locations are rare. This applies in particular on extremely steep shady slopes in high Alpine regions. As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be only a very slight increase in the avalanche danger. These weather conditions will give rise to moistening of the snowpack in some cases at intermediate and high altitudes. In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Below approximately 2800 m: The snowpack is fairly homogeneous and its surface has a melt-freeze crust.
Above approximately 3300 m, shady slopes: The snowpack is dry and its surface consists of loosely bonded snow lying on a crust that is strong in many cases.

As a consequence of highly fluctuating temperatures the snowpack consolidated. This applies in particular on steep sunny slopes at high altitude, and in all aspects at intermediate altitudes.

Below approximately 2300 m only a little snow is lying on south facing slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Further warming. Gradual increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches in the course of the day.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Only isolated wet avalanches are possible. Caution is to be exercised on extremely steep shady slopes. Mostly avalanches are rather small.

Below approximately 2000 m from a snow sport perspective, in most cases insufficient snow is lying.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: The surface of the snowpack will soften during the day.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Friday: The avalanche danger will persist.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: Currently there are favourable conditions mostly. In these regions the avalanche prone locations are rare. As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be only a very slight increase in the avalanche danger. These weather conditions will give rise to slight moistening of the snowpack at intermediate and high altitudes. In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface. This applies in particular in the early morning.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
As a consequence of highly fluctuating temperatures the snowpack consolidated. This applies in particular on steep sunny slopes at high altitude, and in all aspects at intermediate altitudes. The snowpack is fairly homogeneous and its surface has a melt-freeze crust. This applies in particular in the early morning.
Below approximately 2200 m only a little snow is lying on south facing slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Further warming. Gradual increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches in the course of the day.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase a little during the day. The avalanche prone locations are rather rare and are easy to recognise.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Until midday the weather will be clear. Early morning: The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften during the day. In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface. Crampons or ski Crampons are useful, especially on traverses and very smooth and hard slopes.

As a consequence of warming, the activity of moist and wet avalanches will only slowly increase. The near-surface layers of the snowpack can be released in isolated cases by winter sport participants in particular in extremely steep terrain.
In particular in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place and at the base of rock walls only isolated small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized natural wet avalanches are possible.
The conditions are generally favourable for ski touring.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The spring-like weather conditions gave rise to rapid settling of the snowpack.
This situation gave rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2700 m.
The amount of snow is subject to significant local variations.
Especially on sunny slopes below the tree line no snow is lying. On shady slopes below the tree line hardly any snow is lying.
Especially on the colder slopes above the treeline, in the valley basins and headlands that have been less affected by stormy winds in recent weeks, there is excellent snow cover.
Weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack in particular at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: As a consequence of warming during the day small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized moist and wet avalanches are possible below approximately 3300 m.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase a little during the day. The avalanche prone locations are rather rare and are easy to recognise.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Until midday the weather will be clear. Early morning: The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften during the day. In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface. Crampons or ski Crampons are useful, especially on traverses and very smooth and hard slopes.

As a consequence of warming, the activity of moist and wet avalanches will only slowly increase. The near-surface layers of the snowpack can be released in isolated cases by winter sport participants in particular in extremely steep terrain.
In particular in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place and at the base of rock walls only isolated small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized natural wet avalanches are possible.
The conditions are generally favourable for ski touring.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The spring-like weather conditions gave rise to rapid settling of the snowpack.
This situation gave rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2700 m.
The amount of snow is subject to significant local variations.
Especially on sunny slopes below the tree line no snow is lying. On shady slopes below the tree line hardly any snow is lying.
Especially on the colder slopes above the treeline, in the valley basins and headlands that have been less affected by stormy winds in recent weeks, there is excellent snow cover.
Weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack in particular at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: As a consequence of warming during the day small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized moist and wet avalanches are possible below approximately 3300 m.
